title: mesh lane command — provision, list, and auto-name lanes at init
description: Implement the mesh lane subcommand and update mesh init to provision persistent lane worktrees. mesh init should accept --lanes N (default 1) and create N lane worktrees named {user-slug}-{n}, each checked out on a wt/{workspace_id} base branch tracking origin/main. mesh lane list shows each lane's status (idle/active), current branch, and workspace_id. mesh lane add provisions an additional lane on demand. This is the foundation for the worker pool claim model (ADR 0004).
acceptance_criteria: mesh init --lanes N creates N lane worktrees named {user-slug}-{n} adjacent to the shared root
Each lane is checked out on wt/{workspace_id} base branch tracking origin/main
mesh lane list shows workspace_id, status (idle/active), current branch, and worktree path for each lane
mesh lane add {name} provisions an additional lane worktree
Lane names are derived from git config user.name (slugified) + slot index
mesh doctor reports lanes whose wt/ base branch has diverged from origin/main
Tests cover: init creates N lanes, lane list shows correct status, lane add is idempotent
